evidence: The value they get in the first six months will be like... yeah. The value they'll have in six years time if they keep up with it will be wild. Those people won't just be users, they'll be like fans and advocates.
Inverts the entire career tech industry model (which is built on urgency/desperation). Requires 6+ year patience before peak value. The asset becomes the user's life story, not a resume.
Academies have their local towns, regions, catchment areas locked the fuck down. And that is why you can reliably generate talent. If you aren't able to do that, you don't survive as an academy.
The company that 'owns' a university's career pipeline owns 10 years of talent flow from that institution.
Companies will never invest in true AI for resume scanning. Because why would you process a resource where the ratio is like 99 crap for 1% signal. And even that could be totally fake now.
In a world of AI-generated everything, the only reliable signal is direct interaction over time.
Ivor's new Head of Strategic Ops will reach out after joining (week after next). Ivor suggested Handshake unprompted; V confirmed it's the 'actual perfect acquirer'.
Existing product-market fit with career centers; Handshake has distribution to all colleges. V confirmed it's the 'actual perfect acquirer'.

source_display: The Quiet Part Problem as Market Opportunity
evidence: Employers have selection criteria they cannot legally state. The gap between stated job requirements and actual selection preferences is a structural feature of hiring markets, not a bug.

source_display: The Quiet Part Problem as Market Opportunity
evidence: The 'silent scan' becomes more than a feature—it's a philosophical stance about how hiring actually works vs. how it's supposed to work.

source_display: Information Efficiency Has Inverted Recruiter Value
evidence: An Indian recruiter placing US engineers said: 'I have nothing to give these people besides the resume. All of these people look the same.'

source_display: Information Efficiency Has Inverted Recruiter Value
evidence: The standard narrative is that AI threatens recruiters. But this suggests the commoditization already happened—AI just made it visible.

source_display: Response Rate as Only Metric That Matters
evidence: If Careerspan's candidates respond at higher rates than other sources, that's sufficient proof of value. Use response rates as the convincing proxy—'our users pay attention to our recommendations'.

source_display: Response Rate as Only Metric That Matters
evidence: This frames Careerspan as a candidate-centric platform even when selling to employers/recruiters.

source_display: Talent Scanning as Core Value Proposition
evidence: What we are is talent scanning, and that's literally the only thing that matters. This reframes product priorities, marketing messaging, and technical roadmap.

source_display: Talent Scanning as Core Value Proposition
evidence: Ilse's acknowledgment that this is now the go-forward direction.

source_display: Sora Video as Product Vision Artifact
evidence: Ilse's recollection of a generated Sora video—an auditorium full of candidates that empties to three people after clicking a Careerspan button.

source_display: Sora Video as Product Vision Artifact
evidence: The video captured what thousands of words of positioning couldn't: hiring is too loud, and we make it quiet.
Superposition wouldn't bring users. They might not even bring significant revenue. But their logo creates a trust cascade. This is pure signaling economics—the brand IS the product.
Having the name of a prestigious firm on your website may be worth more than their actual business contribution.
V's counter to 'you only have 500 users' was essentially: 'Yes, but they're the right 500.' This inverts the network effects playbook.
Instead of 'we're valuable because we're big,' it's 'we're valuable because we're exclusive.' The Soho House model for talent platforms.

source_display: Cadaver Supply Chain as Disintermediation Analogy
evidence: Logan's biotech friend disrupted her industry by going directly to morgues instead of through middlemen. 'My middleman is charging me too much money' → go direct to source.
Pursue both brand-name partnerships (Superposition) for credibility AND hungry individual recruiters for execution.
Logan owns individual recruiters track; committed 'I am too' to using every connection.
Prioritize New York City for high-signal user acquisition. Channels: Harvard Club of NYC, Cornell Club of NYC, AI Circle (via Albert).
Team agreed to 'use every single connection' for the NYC push. Harvard Club of NYC identified as priority channel.
Cornell Club of NYC identified as priority channel for NYC user acquisition push.
